zh.Harringworth, East Northamptonshire, Nor: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯
英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯
zh.Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de La Zouche於1510年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯時，他的父親John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth25歲，母親Dorothey Capell30歲。他於1550年之前與Lady Margaret Cheney在英國多瑟特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1552年7月22日在他的家鄉享年42歲。

zh-hans.Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England: 英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard de La Zouche于1510年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯时，他的父亲John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth25岁，母亲Dorothey Capell30岁。他于1550年之前与Lady Margaret Cheney在英国多瑟特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1552年7月22日在他的家乡享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Zouche

variants.name: Zaucha
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Casimir, Franciszek, Kazimierz, Krzysztof, Mieczyslaw, Rafal, Wojciech.
Polish: descriptive nickname from za uchem ‘behind the ear’, denoting someone who had something behind one of his ears. Compare Zoucha .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de La Zouche was born in 1510, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, his father, John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth, was 25 and his mother, Dorothey Capell, was 30. He married Lady Margaret Cheney before 1550, in Dorset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 22 July 1552, in his hometown, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard de La Zouche est né en 1510, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Dorothey Capell, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margaret Cheney avant 1550, à Dorset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 22 juillet 1552, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard de La Zouche 1510 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth 25 und seine Mutter Dorothey Capell 30. Er hat vor 1550 in Dorset, England Lady Margaret Cheney geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 22. Juli 1552 im Alter von 42 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de La Zouche è nato il nel 1510 nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Dorothey Capell, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Lady Margaret Cheney prima del 1550, nel Dorset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 22 luglio 1552, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England: イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルス
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de La Zoucheが1510年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスで生まれた時，父John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworthは25歳，母Dorothey Capellは30歳であった。1550年以前にイギリスドールセトでLady Margaret Cheneyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1552年7月22日に故郷で42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de La Zouche(이)가 1510년 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Dorothey Capell(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1550년 이전 영국 도르세트에서 Lady Margaret Cheney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1552년 7월 22일 그의 고향에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de La Zouche nasceu em 1510, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Dorothey Capell, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Margaret Cheney antes 1550, em Dorset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 22 de julho de 1552, em sua cidade natal, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de La Zouche родился в 1510 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothey Capell, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Margaret Cheney перед 1550 г. в местности дорсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 22 июля 1552 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard de La Zouche nació en 1510, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de La Zouche 8th Lord Zouche of Harringworth, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Dorothey Capell, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Lady Margaret Cheney antes de 1550, en Dorset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 22 de julio de 1552, en su pueblo, a la edad de 42 años.
